stories.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot becomes a permanent paid plan on 1 July, with a renewal window closing 30 June
stories.summary: Microsoft confirmed that the Copilot add-ons for Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium become permanent paid plans on 1 July, with the standalone Copilot Business add-on moving to a higher list price at the same time. Customers who renew before 1 July can hold current pricing until their next renewal. Check when your contract renews and what you pay for Copilot, and decide before the deadline rather than letting it roll over.

stories.title: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 with cheaper model tiers, but limits who can use it for now
stories.summary: OpenAI has previewed GPT-5.6 as three tiers, Sol, Terra and Luna, with Terra matching the previous flagship at about half the cost and Luna cheaper still. Access is limited to a small group of partners for now while OpenAI and the US government work through a release process tied to the model's cyber capabilities, with general availability said to be a few weeks away. The takeaway is not to chase a preview you cannot use, but to note the direction: capable AI keeps getting cheaper per task, so once the tiers are generally available it is worth repricing the work you already run on AI and re-checking supplier terms set a year ago.

stories.title: AI agent use is growing fastest among non-technical staff, not engineers
stories.summary: A new OpenAI Economic Research paper tracking a year of Codex use finds the shift from chatbots to agents is moving fastest among non-technical teams, with legal, finance and recruiting at OpenAI making agents their main AI tool and non-developer adoption growing far quicker than developer adoption. The work itself is changing shape, from short questions to delegated tasks that would take a person an hour or more. The prompt for a leader is to look past the engineers and ask which fee-earning and back-office tasks your own people could now hand to an agent, while keeping a human accountable for checking the output.

stories.title: Anthropic put an AI teammate in Slack that anyone can tag and that can act on its own
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, an agent that sits in a Slack channel as a shared team member: anyone can tag it to delegate work, it remembers context from the channels it is in, and an optional ambient mode lets it flag things and chase quiet threads without being asked. It moves AI from a tool you open for one task to something always present in the space where work happens. Before a team starts tagging, decide what it is allowed to see and retain across shared channels, and where it stays reactive rather than acting on its own.

stories.title: The big AI labs are certifying hundreds of thousands of consultants who will soon pitch your firm
stories.summary: OpenAI's Partner Network, launched on 14 June, states an aim to train and certify 300,000 AI consultants by the end of 2026, and Anthropic runs a parallel Services Track for certified partners. Before long, someone holding an OpenAI-certified or Claude-certified badge will pitch your firm for AI help. The badge proves training on a vendor's tools, not that the holder understands your sector's judgement calls or how your people will actually adopt a new way of working, so vet for business judgement, not just tool fluency.
